classes.label: Conference
classes.description: Conference or event described in the conferences table.

classes.label: Organization
classes.description: Organization described in the organizations table.

classes.label: Paper
classes.description: Paper or publication described in the papers table.

classes.label: Person
classes.description: Person described in the persons table.

classes.label: Topic
classes.description: Topic or subject concept described in the topics table.

classes.label: Full Professor
classes.description: Subtype of Person derived from persons.Type = 'Full_Professor'.

classes.label: Researcher
classes.description: Subtype of Person derived from persons.Type = 'Researcher'.

diagnostics.summary: Core entities are well supported by base tables: Conference, Organization, Paper, Person, and Topic. Connector tables are modeled as object properties rather than standalone classes because they primarily consist of foreign keys. Self-references in organizations and topics are modeled as hierarchical object properties.
assumptions: rel_person_paper represents authorship or person-paper association; modeled conservatively as personAuthorOfPaper.
rel_person_organization represents affiliation or membership; modeled conservatively as personAffiliatedWithOrganization.
rel_person_topic and rel_paper_topic represent topical association.
persons.Type contains role-like subtype labels; only observed/explicitly evidenced values Full_Professor and Researcher are proposed as subclasses.
organizations.Type is retained as a literal because subtype vocabulary is not sufficiently evidenced.
papers.Publish is ambiguous and therefore retained only as a low-confidence literal flag.
omissions_and_cautions: No inverse object properties were added, following the conservative modeling guidance.
rel_paper_topic.RelationType was not modeled because its code semantics are not documented in the schema.
Existing URI columns are nullable and do not appear consistently stable enough to use as the sole subject identifier; primary-key-based URI patterns were used for class instances instead.
conference Date is stored as varchar and was therefore modeled as text; Datum was modeled separately as xsd:dateTime.
Tool-derived join evidence was partial for some connector tables, but explicit SQL foreign keys were used where present.
